meanings.definition: a narrative song with a recurrent refrain
a narrative poem of popular origin
put into a certain place or abstract location
examples: Put your things here
Set the tray down
Set the dogs on the scent of the missing children
Place emphasis on a certain point
lay the books on the table
lay the patient carefully onto the bed

meanings.definition: prepare or position for action or operation
examples: lay a fire
lay the foundation for a new health care plan
This hen doesn't lay

meanings.definition: impose as a duty, burden, or punishment
examples: lay a responsibility on someone

meanings.definition: be located or situated somewhere; occupy a certain position
be lying, be prostrate; be in a horizontal position
examples: The sick man lay in bed all day
the books are lying on the shelf
The problems dwell in the social injustices in this country

meanings.definition: be and remain in a particular state or condition
examples: lie dormant

meanings.definition: tell an untruth; pretend with intent to deceive
examples: Don't lie to your parents
She lied when she told me she was only 29

meanings.definition: have a place in relation to something else
examples: The fate of Bosnia lies in the hands of the West
The responsibility rests with the Allies
lie down on the bed until you feel better

meanings.definition: characteristic of those who are not members of the clergy
examples: set his collar in laic rather than clerical position
the lay ministry

meanings.definition: not of or from a profession
examples: a lay opinion as to the cause of the disease
